compiler: Sino-Japanese chant (on'yomi reading of Xuanzang's Chinese)
era: Daily liturgy in Sōtō, Rinzai, Ōbaku, and most Japanese Zen halls
title: Maka Hannya Haramita Shingyō (摩訶般若波羅蜜多心經) — chanted form
content:
## §1 Kaikyō

*The Heart Sūtra as it is sounded in a Japanese Zen meditation hall: the Sino-Japanese (on'yomi) reading of Xuanzang's Chinese, written here in Hepburn romanisation. The Chinese characters are identical to the* 漢 *edition; only the pronunciation differs. Chanted at a steady tempo on a single tone with the* mokugyō *fish-drum keeping the beat.*

Maka hannya haramita shingyō.

Kan-ji-zai bo-satsu, gyō jin hannya haramita ji, shō ken go-un kai kū, do is-sai ku yaku.

## §2 Shiki soku ze kū

Shari-shi, shiki fu-i kū, kū fu-i shiki, shiki soku ze kū, kū soku ze shiki, ju sō gyō shiki, yaku bu nyo ze. Shari-shi, ze sho hō kū sō, fu shō fu metsu, fu ku fu jō, fu zō fu gen.

## §3 Hitei issai sō

Ze ko, kū chū mu shiki, mu ju sō gyō shiki, mu gen ni bi zes-shin ni, mu shiki shō kō mi soku hō, mu gen kai nai-shi mu i-shiki kai. Mu mu-myō, yaku mu mu-myō jin, nai-shi mu rō-shi, yaku mu rō-shi jin. Mu ku shū metsu dō, mu chi yaku mu toku.

## §4 Bo-satsu kukyō nehan

I mu sho-tok-ko, bo-dai-sat-ta, e hannya haramita ko, shin mu kei-ge, mu kei-ge ko, mu u ku-fu, on-ri is-sai ten-dō mu-sō, ku-kyō ne-han. San-ze sho-butsu, e hannya haramita ko, toku a-noku-ta-ra san-myaku san-bo-dai.

## §5 Shingon

Ko chi hannya haramita, ze dai-jin-shu, ze dai-myō-shu, ze mu-jō-shu, ze mu-tō-dō-shu, nō jo is-sai ku, shin-jitsu fu-ko. Ko setsu hannya haramita shu, soku setsu shu watsu:

*Gya-tei gya-tei  hara-gya-tei  hara-sō-gya-tei  bo-ji sowa-ka.*

Hannya shingyō.
locator: Daily Zen sūtra-book chanting form (Sōtōshū / Rinzai-shū liturgy)
edition: Standard Sōtō / Rinzai chant book — public-domain phonetic transcription
